body.title: queue_view.py: compile /mytickets + /opentickets formatting
body.result: queue_view.py compiled; mytickets + opentickets skills now one-liners
body.intention: I intend that /mytickets and /opentickets call a compiled Python script so CC does not re-derive queue formatting logic on each invocation.
body.description:
Currently both skills pipe cc_queue.py output through bash grep filters and CC re-derives the grouping/formatting in prose instructions each time. Move the logic to a compiled script.

Create $CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS/queue_view.py with:
- `--view mytickets` — filter role=guru or worker=akien; group by status (sprint/in_progress/hold); output MY TICKETS shape
- `--view opentickets` — all open tickets; group by status; output totals line

Update ~/.claude/skills/mytickets/SKILL.md and ~/.claude/skills/opentickets/SKILL.md to replace the bash block with `python3 ${CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS}/queue_view.py --view mytickets` (and opentickets respectively). Keep the Output shape description for CC context.

**Affected files:** $CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS/queue_view.py (new), ~/.claude/skills/mytickets/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/skills/opentickets/SKILL.md
**Design rules:** none apply (workflow tooling)
**Scope boundary:** Only mytickets and opentickets. No other skills changed. queue_view.py reads from cc_queue.py's list output or calls its API directly.
**Completion criteria:** `python3 $CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS/queue_view.py --view mytickets` and `--view opentickets` produce correctly grouped output. Skill files contain no inline bash grep pipes.
